Home»Sport»Paris Olympics 2024»NO REST FOR TATJANA: 200m breaststroke schedule at the Paris 2024 Olympics

All you need to know as Tatjana guns for a golden double.

Tatjana Smith aiming to claim a unique slice of history when she lines up in the 100m breaststroke event at the Paris 2024 Olympics.

Smith stands to become only the second swimmer in history after Penny Heyns in 1996 to complete the 100m and 200m breaststroke golden double at the same Olympics event. She wasn’t yet born when Heyns accomplished this historic feat. The 27-year-old contests her 200m breaststroke heat onWednesday 31 July at 11:00 SA timewith thesemi-finals going at 21:51 and 21:58.The finalis onThursday 01 August at 21:04.You know thatNando’s will be watching. Smith won this event in Tokyo with a time of2.18.95, a world record which has since been eclipsed by Russian Evgeniia Chikunova in 2023 with a time of2.17.55.Will Smith have taken that personally?

30-year-old Akani Simbine has tied for fifth-fastest time this year, running 9.86 seconds two weeks before the Olympic Games. 21-year-old Benjamin Richardson and United States-based Shaun Maswanganyi will run with Simbine in the 4x100m event but the fourth member of what should be a formidable quartet is still unclear. Jamaican star Oblique Seville, who has a nod of approval from the iconic Usain Bolt, set the world’s fastest time of 2024, clocking 9.82 seconds in June.

Thepreliminary roundof theMen’s 100m event is on Saturday 03 August at 10:35 SA time. That’s followed byRound 1at11:45.Sunday 04 August sees the semi-finals at 20:00and then thegrand final,where Simbine will hope to line up, at21:50.Don’t miss it for the world, and don’t you love the Olympics?

South Africa has scooped three medals at the games so far. TheMen’s Rugby Sevensside beat Australia to claim bronze, whileAlan Hatherlywon bronze in the men’s Mountain Bike (MTB) cross-country race; South Africa’s first Olympic cycling medal since 1956.Tatjana Smith’s goldin the 100m breaststroke is the highlight.
